description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has just been given access (via a normal, un-privileged shell) to a freshly-provisioned Linux host.  
On this host there already exists a directory that represents a “remote” repository of Kubernetes manifests:

  /home/user/remote_repo/

Your job is to synchronise every file that currently exists in that directory into a new local working directory named

  /home/user/k8s-manifests/

and then write a short, deterministic log that states which files were synchronised.

Detailed functional goals for the session
1. Create the directory /home/user/k8s-manifests/ if it does not already exist.  
2. Copy every regular file that is present directly inside /home/user/remote_repo/ into /home/user/k8s-manifests/.  
   • Copy the files only once; do not descend into sub-directories (there are none at the moment, but the rule must be followed).  
   • After the operation, /home/user/k8s-manifests/ must contain exactly the same filenames and byte-for-byte identical contents as the originals in /home/user/remote_repo/.  
3. Produce a plain-text log file named /home/user/sync.log that lists each file you copied, one file per line, in the exact format shown below.  
   • Each line must use the pattern

         <filename>:SYNCED

     where <filename> is the base name of the file (no leading path).  
   • Put no extra whitespace anywhere in the line.  
   • End every line with a single newline character (␊).  
   • The resulting log must contain a line for every file present in /home/user/remote_repo/ and must contain no additional lines.

Important verification notes  
• File paths and *byte contents* in /home/user/k8s-manifests/ must be identical to those in /home/user/remote_repo/.  
• /home/user/sync.log must exist and conform exactly to the format rules: only “filename:SYNCED”, one per line, newline-terminated, no extra spaces.  
• The task will be graded automatically; therefore any deviation in file names, directory names, or log format will cause failure.  
• You do NOT need super-user privileges. The existing user account has permission to read from /home/user/remote_repo/ and write anywhere inside /home/user/.  

Hints (do NOT copy these commands verbatim; they are only conceptual):  
• A small number of standard GNU/Linux commands (≈3-4) are sufficient: create directory, copy files, list names, redirect list to log.  
• Double-check that the log contains only the base filenames, not full paths.

When you are finished, you should have:
• /home/user/k8s-manifests/nginx-deployment.yaml  
• /home/user/k8s-manifests/nginx-service.yaml  
• /home/user/sync.log containing exactly:

nginx-deployment.yaml:SYNCED  
nginx-service.yaml:SYNCED  

(in either order, each line newline-terminated).

Proceed to the shell and perform the synchronisation.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts
-------------------------------------
Directory          : /home/user/remote_repo/
Files (2)          : 1) nginx-deployment.yaml
                     2) nginx-service.yaml

/home/user/remote_repo/nginx-deployment.yaml
------------------------------------------------
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-deployment
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx:1.21
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/remote_repo/nginx-service.yaml
------------------------------------------------
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: nginx
  ports:
  - protocol: TCP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 80
  type: ClusterIP
------------------------------------------------

Verification rules after agent completes
----------------------------------------
1. Directory /home/user/k8s-manifests/ exists.  
2. /home/user/k8s-manifests/ contains exactly two regular files named
     nginx-deployment.yaml
     nginx-service.yaml
   and their byte content is identical to their counterparts in /home/user/remote_repo/.
3. /home/user/sync.log exists, is a regular file, and its content is either:
     nginx-deployment.yaml:SYNCED\nnginx-service.yaml:SYNCED\n
   OR
     nginx-service.yaml:SYNCED\nnginx-deployment.yaml:SYNCED\n
   (i.e., the two required lines, each terminated with a single newline, in any order).
name: task_000000_922ac376
